rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege facts supporting that Defendant acted in order to affect Plaintiff, not just with the awareness that their actions would affect Plaintiff. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is sustained in part and overruled in part: Defendant's MJOP to Plaintiff's First Cause of Action for Judicial Foreclosure should be SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting that a cause of action exists as to Defendant First American. Defendant's MJOP to Plaintiff's Second Cause of Action for Slander of Title should be OVERRULED. Defendant makes no argument that Plaintiff has failed to allege facts supporting the elements for slander of title. Defendant's MJOP to Counts One and Two of Plaintiff's Fourth Cause of Action for Negligence should be SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting that First American intentionally did not apprise Plaintiff of the 2019 proceedings so as to subordinate Plaintiff's interest. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: THE INFINITY OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CONDOMINIUM VS. JAMES GUTIERREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. The Notice of Motion does not specify what the summary judgment is being sought on (complaint, cross-complaint or both), the Separate Statement does not contain facts that purport to support a summary judgment on the complaint. Motion for Summary Adjudication on Affirmative defenses and the cross-complaint is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION BY SALE OF REAL PROPERTY, FOR APPOINTMENT OF PARTITION REFEREE, FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER, AND FOR OTHER RELIEF is continued to January 16, 2023. No later than January 8, 2024 by 4:p.m. Plaintiff to file a proof of service for the Order Shortening Time entered on December 20, 2023. The court notes that Plaintiff filed a reply to an opposition. There is no opposition on file. rulings.Case Title: JAMES CHAFFEE VS. ARLENE A. SCHELLER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 6. DEFENDANT MYND MANAGEMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the causes of action for wrongful eviction and elder abuse is overruled as the moving party failed to carry their burden and show that complaint fails to state facts to support these causes of action. Legal authorities cited in the relevant sections of the demurrer are no analyzed, the missing elements are not discussed, etc. Demurrer to the causes of action for fraud, Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress for plaintiff to allege facts supporting all elements of these causes of action, including but not limited to the details of the false promise, intent to induce reliance, actual reliance; outrageous conduct; duty and breach of duty. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deposition Responses From Laura Douglass Decarava And For Reasonable Sanctions Against Attorney Jacqueline Patton Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 7. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION RESPONSES FROM LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA AND FOR REASONABLE SANCTIONS AGAINST ATTORNEY JACQUELINE PATTON is OFF CALENDAR. No evidence of good faith meet and confer efforts provided. An e-mail attached to the Request for Judicial Notice is not admissible evidence. Even if it were, the e-mail was purportedly sent at 7:07 p.m. on December 20, 2023 and the motion was filed 4 business hours later, at noon on December 21, 2023. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $4,656 to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: THE 300 COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP VS. DIGITIAL REVOLUTION MEDIA CENTER LLC A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 8. PLAINTIFF THE 300 COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL PARTNERSHIP'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party seeks to attach past due rent, holdover damages, CAM charges, estimated costs and attorney's fees. There is no evidence to support the costs and fees request and no basis to include the CAM charges in this unlawful detainer based on a Notice seeking rent through August 31, 2023 and holdover damages. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 8, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ROBERT WILSON'S MOTION FOR IMPROPER SERVICE This appears to be an opposition to Plaintiff's pending request for service of Summons and Complaint via posting and mailing.
